serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant serving of approximately 200 g, comprising a plate of mesclun greens, crispy seasonal vegetables, Gaeta olives, and balsamic-EVO dressing.

nutrient_source.title: Salad greens, raw; Olives, ripe, canned; Olive oil; Balsamic vinegar — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Foundation Foods
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry exists for this specific restaurant dish. Nutrient values estimated by weighted-average component analysis using USDA FoodData Central data for mesclun/mixed salad greens (FDC 169248), Gaeta-style ripe olives (FDC 169094), extra virgin olive oil (FDC 171413), and balsamic vinegar (FDC 173469). Estimated composition: ~60% mixed greens and seasonal vegetables, ~15% Gaeta olives, ~15% olive oil dressing, ~10% balsamic vinegar. Values represent a plausible per-100g profile for the assembled dish.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — mixed salad with olive oil dressing analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific Italian vegan salad. GI estimated at 32 based on analogous mixed green salad with olive oil dressing entries in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. Mixed leafy greens and non-starchy vegetables consistently yield GI values in the 15–40 range; olive oil and balsamic vinegar contribute negligible glycemic load. GL per serving calculated as (32 × 6.1 × 200/100) / 100 = 3.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from Gaeta olives and balsamic dressing
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be aware that Gaeta olives contribute approximately 210 mg sodium per 100 g of this dish; portion control is advisable, and requesting reduced olive quantity or dressing on the side can lower sodium intake meaningfully.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index and low glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of approximately 32 and a glycemic load of ~3.9 per serving, this salad is well-suited for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes as it produces minimal postprandial blood glucose elevation; the olive oil content also supports insulin sensitivity.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High monounsaturated fat from extra virgin olive oil; zero cholesterol
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The predominance of monounsaturated fatty acids (~6.5 g/100g) from organic extra virgin olive oil, combined with zero cholesterol and high polyphenol content from balsamic vinegar and olives, makes this dish cardioprotective and consistent with Mediterranean dietary guidelines for cardiovascular risk reduction.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Vegana is a fully vegan Italian salad served at Ricciotti restaurant in Singapore, composed of mesclun salad greens, crispy seasonal vegetables, Gaeta olives, organic extra virgin olive oil (EVO), and balsamic dressing. The dish is raw and minimally processed, assembled rather than cooked, and reflects the Italian tradition of simple, high-quality ingredient-led salads. Mesclun is a mix of young tender salad leaves including arugula, frisée, radicchio, and spinach, providing a slightly bitter and peppery flavour profile. Gaeta olives are small, dark Italian olives with a mild, fruity, and slightly salty taste. The balsamic dressing adds a sweet-acidic complexity. Nutritionally, the dish is low in carbohydrates and calories (~112 kcal/100g), rich in monounsaturated fats from olive oil, and provides dietary fibre, vitamin A, vitamin C, folate, and iron from the greens and vegetables. It is gluten-free, dairy-free, and suitable for vegan and halal diets. The glycemic index is low (~32), making it appropriate for blood glucose management. The sodium content (~210 mg/100g) is moderate due to the olives and dressing, warranting attention for hypertensive individuals. This dish is clinically relevant as a low-GI, anti-inflammatory, Mediterranean-style salad that supports cardiovascular health and weight management.
common_user_queries: Mesclun salad with balsamic dressing nutrition

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry exists for this specific restaurant dish. Values are estimated by weighted-average component analysis using USDA FoodData Central approved sources for the identified ingredients. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for comparable mixed green salads with olive oil dressing. Confidence is medium rather than low because the ingredient list is explicit and the component sources are high-quality approved references.
notes: Dish served exclusively at Ricciotti - The Riverwalk, Singapore (field survey). The food_group has been reclassified from 'Grains and staples' (input) to 'Vegetables' as the dish is a salad with no grain component. The cuisine_tag 'Chicken Dish' in the input is erroneous for this vegan dish and has been disregarded. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero as extra virgin olive oil and plant-based ingredients contain no industrially produced trans fats (naturally zero). vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero as this is a fully plant-based dish with no animal-derived ingredients. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero as no vitamin-D-containing ingredients (animal products, fortified foods) are present. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero as all ingredients are plant-derived. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data and are not individually reported by a single source entry. No -1 sentinel values are used in this record as all fields have been estimated from component data. Italian cuisine does not require Singaporean multilingual naming (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi); those local_names fields are null by design as this is an Italian dish, not a Singaporean dish. GI category 'low' is consistent with glycemic_index = 32 (≤ 55 threshold).
